# X Algorithm Deep-Dive -- Generated by Claude

> I reverse-engineered the open-source X "For You" algorithm repo (May 2026 release). Here's everything I found about how the algorithm actually works, what it favors, and how to maximize your reach.

---

## Thread (1/20)

I went through the entire X algorithm source code -- Phoenix (Grok-based transformer), Thunder (in-network), Home Mixer (orchestration), and Grox (content safety).

Here's how X actually decides what you see in your For You feed.

A thread based on real production code.

---

## (2/20) The Pipeline

Every tweet in your For You feed survives a 10-stage gauntlet:

1. Query Hydration (who are you?)
2. Candidate Sourcing (Thunder + Phoenix)
3. Hydration (enrich metadata)
4. Pre-Score Filtering (kill ineligible)
5. Phoenix Scoring (Grok transformer)
6. Weighted Scoring (combine predictions)
7. Author Diversity (decay repeats)
8. OON Adjustment (in vs out-of-network)
9. Top-K Selection
10. Post-Selection Safety Filter

---

## (3/20) The Core Formula

The ranking formula is literally:

**Final Score = SUM(weight_i x P(action_i))**

Phoenix predicts 15+ engagement probabilities per tweet per viewer.

Positive actions (like, reply, repost, share) have positive weights.
Negative actions (block, mute, report) have negative weights.

Your tweet is scored independently for EACH viewer.

---

## (4/20) The 15 Signals That Boost Your Tweet

The algorithm tracks these positive signals, each with its own weight:

- P(favorite) -- likes
- P(reply) -- replies
- P(repost) -- reposts
- P(quote) -- quote tweets
- P(share) -- general shares
- P(share_via_DM) -- DM shares
- P(share_via_copy_link) -- link copies
- P(click) -- expanding tweet
- P(profile_click) -- visiting your profile
- P(photo_expand) -- expanding images
- P(video_quality_view) -- watching video
- P(dwell) -- stopped scrolling
- P(dwell_time) -- how long they read
- P(click_dwell_time) -- time after clicking in
- P(follow_author) -- following you

---

## (5/20) The 5 Signals That KILL Your Tweet

These negative signals have negative weights and actively push your content DOWN:

- P(not_interested) -- tapped "Not interested"
- P(block_author) -- blocked you
- P(mute_author) -- muted you
- P(report) -- reported the tweet
- P(not_dwelled) -- scrolled past without stopping

If the model predicts a viewer would take these actions, your tweet gets buried BEFORE they even see it.

---

## (6/20) Why Multi-Signal Engagement Wins

A tweet with 100 likes scores LOWER than a tweet with 50 likes + 20 replies + 10 reposts + 5 DM shares.

Why? Because each engagement type adds independently to the score.

The formula is a SUM across all weighted predictions. More signal types = higher combined score.

Stop optimizing only for likes.

---

## (7/20) The Share Multiplier Nobody Talks About

X tracks THREE separate share signals:

1. General share (share_score)
2. Share via DM (share_via_dm_score)
3. Share via copy link (share_via_copy_link_score)

Each has its own weight. Content that gets DM'd AND link-copied gets a triple share boost that text-only-liked content can never match.

Create "you need to see this" content.

---

## (8/20) Video Gets Exclusive Scoring

Videos have a dedicated VQV (Video Quality View) signal that text and images CANNOT access.

But here's the catch from the code:

```
if video_duration_ms > MIN_VIDEO_DURATION_MS {
    vqv_weight  // bonus applied
} else {
    0.0  // zero, nothing
}
```

Short clips below the threshold get ZERO video bonus. Make your videos count.

---

## (9/20) Dwell Time Is Real

The algorithm measures:

1. **Binary dwell** -- did you stop scrolling? (dwell_score)
2. **How long you read** -- continuous time (dwell_time)
3. **Time after clicking in** -- (click_dwell_time)

All three contribute independently.

Write longer, substantive posts. Use threads. Include details that reward careful reading. The algorithm literally measures reading time.

---

## (10/20) The Follow Signal

P(follow_author) is directly in the scoring formula.

The model predicts: "If this viewer sees this tweet, will they follow the author?"

Content from accounts that consistently convert impressions to follows gets algorithmically boosted.

Your bio, pinned tweet, and posting consistency all feed this signal.

---

## (11/20) Author Diversity = Diminishing Returns

The code implements exponential decay for repeated authors:

**multiplier = (1 - floor) x decay^position + floor**

- Your 1st tweet in a feed: multiplier = 1.0 (full score)
- Your 2nd tweet: multiplied by ~decay
- Your 3rd tweet: multiplied by decay^2
- And so on...

Posting 10 tweets/day means each one competes with itself. Quality > quantity.

---

## (12/20) In-Network vs Out-of-Network

Your followers see you via Thunder (in-network, full score).

Non-followers see you via Phoenix retrieval (out-of-network, multiplied by OON_WEIGHT_FACTOR).

OON content is structurally harder to rank.

BUT: new users get a special NEW_USER_OON_WEIGHT_FACTOR boost if they follow enough accounts. The algorithm helps new creators grow.

---

## (13/20) Your Engagement History IS Your Identity

Phoenix processes your last 128 engagement actions as a sequence fed to a Grok-based transformer.

The model doesn't see your username. It sees:
- What you liked, replied to, reposted
- Which authors you engaged with
- How long you dwelled
- What you blocked/muted

Regular posting builds consistent engagement patterns. Irregular posting breaks the signal.

---

## (14/20) Grox: The AI Quality Gate

Before ranking, the Grox pipeline uses Grok VLM to evaluate content:

- **BangerInitialScreen**: assigns quality_score (0-1). Below 0.4 = flagged.
- **SpamClassifier**: low-follower accounts get extra scrutiny
- **SafetyPTOS**: checks 7 policy categories
- **ReplyScorer**: ranks reply quality 0-3
- **slop_score**: yes, they literally detect AI slop

High-quality original content passes. Generic/spammy content doesn't.

---

## (15/20) The Filters That Kill Before Scoring

Your tweet gets removed BEFORE scoring if:

- Duplicate post ID
- Too old (AgeFilter)
- Your own tweet (SelfpostFilter)
- From blocked/muted author
- Contains muted keywords
- Previously seen by this user
- Already served in this session
- Ineligible subscription content
- Repost of already-shown content

Many reach losses happen here, not at ranking.

---

## (16/20) Safety Kills Reach Post-Ranking

Even if you score high, VFFilter removes posts flagged for:

- Spam
- Violence/gore
- Adult content
- Hate/abuse
- Illegal behaviors
- Violent speech
- Suicide/self-harm

Brand safety also tiers content: Safe > LowRisk > MediumRisk.

MediumRisk = no ads placed near you = less distribution.

---

## (17/20) Mutual Follow Networks Matter

The algorithm calculates Jaccard similarity between your followers and a viewer's followers using MinHash (256 hash values).

Higher mutual follower overlap = higher relevance for out-of-network discovery.

Engage with communities in your niche. Follow and interact with accounts whose followers overlap with your target audience.

---

## (18/20) Post Age = Time Decay

Phoenix uses post_age_buckets:

- Max: 4800 minutes (~80 hours)
- Granularity: 60-minute buckets
- Thunder retention: 2 days

The model learns time-dependent engagement patterns. A post that gets strong engagement in its first hour signals relevance and compounds.

Post when your audience is active. The first 60 minutes are critical.

---

## (19/20) The Complete Playbook

**DO:**
1. Trigger multiple engagement types (likes + replies + reposts + shares)
2. Post qualifying-length video for VQV bonus
3. Maximize dwell time with substantive content
4. Drive DM shares and copy-link shares (3 separate signals)
5. Optimize for the follow action
6. Post consistently to build engagement history patterns
7. Stay in your niche for retrieval model clarity

---

## (20/20) The Don'ts

**DON'T:**
1. Trigger blocks/mutes/reports (strongest negative weights)
2. Get flagged by Grox safety classifiers
3. Post low-quality content (quality < 0.4 = flagged, slop detection is real)
4. Flood with posts (author diversity decay is exponential)
5. Create scroll-past content (not_dwelled trains the model against you)
6. Post short throwaway videos (VQV weight = 0.0 below duration threshold)
7. Ignore early engagement (first 60 min bucket matters most)

---

*Generated by Claude -- based on source code analysis of the X algorithm repository (May 2026 release). Components: home-mixer/ (Rust), phoenix/ (Python/JAX), thunder/ (Rust), grox/ (Python), candidate-pipeline/ (Rust).*
